violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for driving or riding a two-wheeler without wearing a protective headgear where required by law.

penalty.licence_suspension: Disqualification of licence for three months

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor cycle or causes or allows a motor cycle to be driven without wearing protective headgear... shall be punishable with fine of one thousand rupees and he shall be disqualified for licence for three months

title.en: Driving without a valid driving licence

violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a motor vehicle without a valid driving licence or in contravention of licence conditions.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor vehicle in contravention of section 3 or section 4 shall be punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months or with fine of five thousand rupees, or with both

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for driving a motor vehicle at a speed exceeding the posted or prescribed speed limit.

penalty.other_penalty_text: For Light Motor Vehicle (LMV): first offence ₹1,000–₹2,000; Medium/Heavy vehicle: ₹2,000–₹4,000

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor vehicle in contravention of the speed limits referred to in section 112 shall be punishable for the first offence with a fine of one thousand rupees or for any subsequent offence with a fine of two thousand rupees
extraction_notes: LMV first offence ₹1,000–₹2,000; HMV first offence ₹2,000–₹4,000 per amended schedule

title.en: Driving under influence of alcohol or drugs

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for driving a motor vehicle with blood alcohol concentration exceeding the prescribed limit, or under the influence of drugs.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or both
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or both

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor vehicle, or attempts to drive a motor vehicle, while under the influence of alcohol, having alcohol exceeding 30 mg. per 100 ml. of blood... shall be punishable for the first offence with imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine of ten thousand rupees
extraction_notes: Non-compoundable offence — cannot pay and settle roadside

violations.rule_reference: Rule 138(3)

title.en: Not wearing seat belt while driving

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for the driver and passengers of a motor vehicle for not wearing seat belts as required by motor vehicle rules.
violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever contravenes the provisions of section 138(3) [seat belts]... shall be punishable with fine of one thousand rupees

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for driving a motor vehicle in a public place without a valid third-party insurance policy as required by law.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months, or both
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months, or both

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor vehicle or causes or allows a motor vehicle to be driven in a public place without there being in force in relation to the use of the vehicle a policy of insurance... shall be punishable with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine of two thousand rupees

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for driving a motor vehicle that has not been registered or whose registration has expired.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with both
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with both

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor vehicle or causes or allows a motor vehicle to be driven in contravention of... (registration) shall be punishable for the first offence with a fine of five thousand rupees or imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for driving a motor vehicle on a public road in a manner that is dangerous to the public, having regard to all circumstances.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with both
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with both

penalty.licence_disqualification: Disqualification from holding or obtaining a driving licence
penalty.community_service: Community service may be ordered by court

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor vehicle at a speed or in a manner which is dangerous to the public... shall be punishable for the first offence with imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months or with fine of one thousand rupees to five thousand rupees
extraction_notes: Non-compoundable — must appear in court

title.en: Violation of traffic signal or sign

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for contravening any traffic signal, road sign, or traffic regulation not covered by a specific section.
violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever contravenes any provision of this Act or of any rule... for which no penalty is provided in this Act... shall be punishable for the first offence with a fine of five hundred rupees

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for using a hand-held communication device, including a mobile phone, while driving a motor vehicle.

penalty.licence_suspension: Suspension of driving licence for three months

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever drives a motor vehicle while using a hand-held communication device... shall be punishable with a fine of one thousand rupees... and his driving licence shall be suspended for a period of three months
extraction_notes: 2019 Amendment introduced this specific section

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty where a minor drives a motor vehicle; the registered owner or guardian is held liable, and the vehicle registration may be cancelled.

imprisonment.text: Guardian or owner shall be liable for imprisonment of 3 years

penalty.other_penalty_text: Vehicle registration may be cancelled. Juvenile tried under Juvenile Justice Act.

violations.source_text_excerpt: where a motor vehicle is driven by a juvenile, the guardian of the juvenile or the owner of the vehicle shall be deemed to have committed an offence punishable with imprisonment of 3 years and fine of twenty-five thousand rupees... the registration of the vehicle shall be cancelled
extraction_notes: Guardian / registered owner is penalised, not the juvenile

title.en: Driving without valid Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificate

violations.plain_english_summary: Penalty for driving a motor vehicle without a valid Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificate as required by emission norms.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or both
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or both

violations.source_text_excerpt: whoever uses or causes or allows to be used in any public place a motor vehicle which does not comply with the standards prescribed in relation to road safety... shall be punishable with a fine of one thousand rupees

violations.rule_reference: Driving at excessive speed
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving faster than the official speed limit.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving faster than the official speed limit.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Penalty for not wearing protective headgear
violations.plain_english_summary: Riding a two-wheeler without wearing a helmet or carrying a pillion rider without a helmet.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Riding a two-wheeler without wearing a helmet or carrying a pillion rider without a helmet.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Use of safety belts and the seating of children
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a car without wearing a seatbelt or ferrying passengers not wearing seatbelts.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a car without wearing a seatbelt or ferrying passengers not wearing seatbelts.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Driving uninsured vehicle
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a vehicle without a valid third-party insurance cover.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a vehicle without a valid third-party insurance cover.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Driving vehicles in contravention of section 3 or section 4
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a vehicle without holding an effective and valid driving licence.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a vehicle without holding an effective and valid driving licence.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Offences by Juveniles
violations.plain_english_summary: A minor driving a motor vehicle and committing an offence.
violations.source_text_excerpt: A minor driving a motor vehicle and committing an offence.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Failure to allow free passage to emergency vehicles
violations.plain_english_summary: Failing to give way to fire engines, ambulances, or other emergency vehicles.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Failing to give way to fire engines, ambulances, or other emergency vehicles.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Using vehicle in unsafe condition

title.en: Violation of road safety and pollution standards

violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a vehicle violating standards for road safety, control of noise, or air pollution (e.g. without PUC certificate).
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a vehicle violating standards for road safety, control of noise, or air pollution (e.g. without PUC certificate).
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Using vehicle without permit
violations.plain_english_summary: Using a transport vehicle without a valid permit.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Using a transport vehicle without a valid permit.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Using vehicle without registration
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a motor vehicle without a valid registration certificate.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a motor vehicle without a valid registration certificate.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Driving vehicle exceeding permissible weight
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a transport vehicle loaded beyond the legally permissible limits.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a transport vehicle loaded beyond the legally permissible limits.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Carriage of excess passengers
violations.plain_english_summary: Carrying more passengers than officially permitted in a transport vehicle.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Carrying more passengers than officially permitted in a transport vehicle.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: General provision for punishment of offences
violations.plain_english_summary: General penalty for breaking any traffic rule where no specific penalty is provided.
violations.source_text_excerpt: General penalty for breaking any traffic rule where no specific penalty is provided.
extraction_notes: Auto-extracted from Model

violations.rule_reference: Rule 50 / 51 Central Motor Vehicles Rules
violations.plain_english_summary: Using registration plates that do not conform to the prescribed font, size, or display (fancy fonts, names, or non-HSRP plates in some zones).

penalty.other_penalty_text: May be combined with Section 192 for unregistered/HSRP violations in some states.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Using registration plates that do not conform to the prescribed font, size, or display (fancy fonts, names, or non-HSRP plates in some zones).
extraction_notes: Commonly penalized as 500 for first time under general section.

violations.rule_reference: Driving against the flow of traffic
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a vehicle in the opposite direction of the traffic flow or entering a 'No Entry' or 'One Way' street incorrectly.

penalty.other_penalty_text: If driving is deemed 'dangerous' to others, police may book under Section 184 (₹1000-₹5000).

violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a vehicle in the opposite direction of the traffic flow or entering a 'No Entry' or 'One Way' street incorrectly.
extraction_notes: Baseline is 177A, but frequently escalated to 184.

violations.rule_reference: Using vehicle without permit
violations.plain_english_summary: Using a transport vehicle in any public place without a valid permit issued by the Authority.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment up to 6 months
Imprisonment up to 1 year

violations.source_text_excerpt: Using a transport vehicle in any public place without a valid permit issued by the Authority.
extraction_notes: One of the highest fines in the Act for commercial non-compliance.

violations.rule_reference: Rule 105 CMVR

title.en: Improper Use of High Beam / Dazzling Lights

violations.plain_english_summary: Using high beams in a manner that dazzles other drivers, or using unauthorized multi-colored or excessively bright auxiliary lights.

penalty.other_penalty_text: Illegal auxiliary lights may be confiscated/removed on the spot.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Using high beams in a manner that dazzles other drivers, or using unauthorized multi-colored or excessively bright auxiliary lights.
extraction_notes: Penalty falls under general Section 177.

violations.rule_reference: Using vehicle in unsafe condition
violations.plain_english_summary: Using a vehicle that is in such a condition that it is a danger to the public (e.g., failed brakes, bald tires, hanging parts).

penalty.licence_suspension: Registration may be suspended until repair.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Using a vehicle that is in such a condition that it is a danger to the public (e.g., failed brakes, bald tires, hanging parts).
extraction_notes: Distinct from Pollution (190(2)).

violations.rule_reference: Noise pollution and silencer modification
violations.plain_english_summary: Using a modified silencer, high-decibel air horns, or excessive honking in silent zones.

penalty.other_penalty_text: Modified silencers (e.g. on Bullets) are frequently confiscated.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Using a modified silencer, high-decibel air horns, or excessive honking in silent zones.
extraction_notes: Combined enforcement with 190(2) usually results in ₹1000+ fines.

violations.rule_reference: Violation of High Security Registration Plate (HSRP)
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a vehicle without the High Security Registration Plate mandated by the government for all old and new vehicles.

penalty.other_penalty_text: Fine amount varies by state implementation of Section 192.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a vehicle without the High Security Registration Plate mandated by the government for all old and new vehicles.
extraction_notes: Heavily enforced in DL, KA, MH.

violations.rule_reference: Rule 100 CMVR
violations.plain_english_summary: Using sun-film or tinting glass beyond permissible limits (must be 70% VLT for front/back and 50% for sides).

penalty.other_penalty_text: Police usually strip off the film on the spot in addition to the fine.

violations.source_document: Supreme Court Order Avishek Goenka vs Union Of India
violations.source_text_excerpt: Using sun-film or tinting glass beyond permissible limits (must be 70% VLT for front/back and 50% for sides).
extraction_notes: SC order strictly prohibits ANY film, even if light.

violations.rule_reference: Avishek Goenka vs Union of India
violations.plain_english_summary: Carrying a secondary rider (pillion) on a motorcycle who is not wearing a protective helmet.

penalty.licence_suspension: Disqualification of rider's licence for three months.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Carrying a secondary rider (pillion) on a motorcycle who is not wearing a protective helmet.
extraction_notes: Applies to the rider even if they are wearing one but the pillion isn't.

violations.rule_reference: Failure to draw to the side of the road
violations.plain_english_summary: Failure to allow free passage to emergency vehicles like ambulances, fire engines, or other responder vehicles using sirens.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment up to 6 months

violations.source_text_excerpt: Failure to allow free passage to emergency vehicles like ambulances, fire engines, or other responder vehicles using sirens.
extraction_notes: New section introduced in 2019 with heavy fine.

violations.rule_reference: Penalty for violation of safety measures for motor cycle and pillion riders
violations.plain_english_summary: Carrying more than one person in addition to the driver on a two-wheeled motorcycle.

penalty.licence_suspension: Disqualification for holding licence for three months.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Carrying more than one person in addition to the driver on a two-wheeled motorcycle.
extraction_notes: Directly applies to the registered owner/driver.

violations.rule_reference: Parking in prohibited area / causing obstruction
violations.plain_english_summary: Parking a vehicle in a 'No Parking' zone, on a footpath, near a hydrant, or in a manner that obstructs traffic flow or visibility.

penalty.other_penalty_text: Towing charges are usually extra (₹200-₹1000 depending on vehicle).

violations.source_text_excerpt: Parking a vehicle in a 'No Parking' zone, on a footpath, near a hydrant, or in a manner that obstructs traffic flow or visibility.
extraction_notes: Base penalty is 500, but obstruction (122) can be cited.

violations.rule_reference: Power to restrict the use of vehicles
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a vehicle into a road or area where entry is prohibited by a official sign or notification.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a vehicle into a road or area where entry is prohibited by a official sign or notification.
extraction_notes: Fines vary by city traffic police notifications.

violations.rule_reference: Driving dangerously
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving in a zig-zag manner on a public road, which is considered dangerous to the public.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment or fine
Imprisonment or fine

violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving in a zig-zag manner on a public road, which is considered dangerous to the public.
extraction_notes: Explicitly listed as a dangerous activity in the 2019 amendment.

violations.rule_reference: Violation of stop line at traffic signal
violations.plain_english_summary: Stopping or crossing the white 'stop line' painted on the road when the traffic signal is red.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Stopping or crossing the white 'stop line' painted on the road when the traffic signal is red.
extraction_notes: One of the most common automated e-challan types.

violations.rule_reference: Crossing solid yellow or white line
violations.plain_english_summary: Crossing a solid yellow or white line on the road which indicates 'no overtaking' or 'fixed lanes'.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Crossing a solid yellow or white line on the road which indicates 'no overtaking' or 'fixed lanes'.
extraction_notes: Often cited as lane indiscipline.

violations.rule_reference: Plate numbers not clearly visible
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving with a number plate that is dirty, broken, hidden, or where the characters are not clearly legible.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving with a number plate that is dirty, broken, hidden, or where the characters are not clearly legible.
extraction_notes: Common during monsoon (muddy plates) or night checks.

violations.rule_reference: Penalty for refusal to give information
violations.plain_english_summary: Refusing to provide your name, address, or licence details when lawfully required by a police officer.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Refusing to provide your name, address, or licence details when lawfully required by a police officer.
extraction_notes: Specific sub-section for withholding info.

violations.rule_reference: Racing and trials of speed
violations.plain_english_summary: Engaging in any race or trial of speed between motor vehicles in any public place without written consent from the State Government.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment up to 1 month
Imprisonment up to 1 month

violations.source_text_excerpt: Engaging in any race or trial of speed between motor vehicles in any public place without written consent from the State Government.
extraction_notes: Non-compoundable; requires court appearance.

violations.rule_reference: Offences relating to driving licences
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a motor vehicle while still disqualified from holding or obtaining a driving licence by a court or authority.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment up to 3 months

violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a motor vehicle while still disqualified from holding or obtaining a driving licence by a court or authority.
extraction_notes: High penalty to deter violation of court orders.

violations.rule_reference: Air horn misuse and silent zones
violations.plain_english_summary: Using multi-toned or air-pressure horns that emit excessive noise, or honking in designated 'Silent Zones' (near hospitals, schools, etc.).

penalty.other_penalty_text: The prohibited horn may be confiscated by the officer.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Using multi-toned or air-pressure horns that emit excessive noise, or honking in designated 'Silent Zones' (near hospitals, schools, etc.).
extraction_notes: Heavily enforced as part of noise pollution control.

violations.rule_reference: Needless honking in silent zone
violations.plain_english_summary: Honking without any reasonable necessity or continuous sounding of the horn, especially in a silent zone.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Honking without any reasonable necessity or continuous sounding of the horn, especially in a silent zone.
extraction_notes: Introduced specifically to combat urban noise pollution.

violations.rule_reference: Safety of children in motor vehicles
violations.plain_english_summary: Failure to ensure that a child below the age of 14 is secured by a safety belt or appropriate child restraint system.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Failure to ensure that a child below the age of 14 is secured by a safety belt or appropriate child restraint system.
extraction_notes: Holds the adult driver responsible for the child's safety.

violations.rule_reference: Penalty for causing obstruction to free flow of traffic
violations.plain_english_summary: Leaving a vehicle in a public place in such a manner as to cause obstruction to the free flow of traffic or danger to others.

penalty.other_penalty_text: Plus ₹500 for every hour during which such obstruction continues.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Leaving a vehicle in a public place in such a manner as to cause obstruction to the free flow of traffic or danger to others.
extraction_notes: A common way to penalize vehicles parked on main roads for long durations.

violations.rule_reference: Allowing person without licence to drive
violations.plain_english_summary: A vehicle owner allowing a person who does not hold a valid licence or a minor to drive their motor vehicle.

imprisonment.text: Imprisonment up to 3 months

penalty.other_penalty_text: If the person is a minor, see Section 199A for much higher penalties (₹25000).

violations.source_text_excerpt: A vehicle owner allowing a person who does not hold a valid licence or a minor to drive their motor vehicle.
extraction_notes: Owner is legally responsible for verifying the driver's licence.

violations.rule_reference: Driving without indicators or defective lamps
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a vehicle with non-functional indicators, tail lamps, or brake lights, making it dangerous for other road users.
violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a vehicle with non-functional indicators, tail lamps, or brake lights, making it dangerous for other road users.
extraction_notes: One of the most frequent safety-check failures.

violations.rule_reference: Driving when mentally or physically unfit
violations.plain_english_summary: Driving a motor vehicle by a person with a known disease or disability which makes their driving a source of danger to themselves or others.

penalty.licence_suspension: Immediate suspension and medical review of licence.

violations.source_text_excerpt: Driving a motor vehicle by a person with a known disease or disability which makes their driving a source of danger to themselves or others.
extraction_notes: Applies to epilepsy, severe vision loss, or other certified disabilities.
